articles.description:
Wendy's is in the process of quietly switching from whole-leafed romaine lettuce to shredded iceberg lettuce for its burgers and chicken sandwiches. The switch began this summer at some locations and recently appeared here in Los Angeles.

The move was reportedly made to improve lettuce consistency and maintain a reliable supply. Where the romaine lettuce was hand-leafed at each location, the shredded iceberg lettuce arrives already prepared in bags, so there's a convenience factor as well.
The lettuce change does not affect the chain's salads, which, depending on the salad, are made with romaine and/or spring mix.
Personal preferences between shredded lettuce and leafed lettuce on a burger/sandwich can vary. Generally, leaf lettuce tends to wilt faster as there's typically more contact between the burger patty and the lettuce, while shredded lettuce occupies more volume, which provides more of a buffer against heat. However, shredded lettuce is also a lot messier as it falls out of your burger/sandwich easily. It's also cheaper.
Additionally, for anyone who was getting lettuce-wrapped burgers, that's no longer an option at locations that have made the switch.
Wendy's has not yet updated its website to reflect the change as of this writing (pictured). I recently got a burger from my local Wendy's and definitely noticed the shredded lettuce, but didn't note any mention of it during my visit.
Photo via Wendy's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Wendy's Switching to Shredded Lettuce in Burgers

articles.description:
Chicken Finger Tacos are back at Zaxbys and have brought along a new flavor: Buffalo Blue.

The new Buffalo Blue Taco features a hand-breaded chicken finger, pico de gallo, blue cheese crumbles, shredded iceberg lettuce, and a drizzle of Buffalo Garlic Blaze sauce and Ranch, served folded in a warm flour tortilla.
The new taco is joined by the returning Chipotle Ranch with Pico Taco and Avocado Ranch BLT Taco.
The Chipotle Ranch with Pico Taco includes a hand-breaded chicken finger, shredded iceberg lettuce, cheddar jack cheese, Pico de Gallo, and mildly spicy Chipotle Ranch sauce, in a warm flour tortilla.
The Avocado Ranch BLT Taco features a hand-breaded chicken finger, sliced Roma tomatoes, hardwood-smoked bacon, shredded iceberg lettuce, and a drizzle of Avocado Ranch, served folded in a warmed flour tortilla.
Chicken Finger Tacos are available for a limited time now through November 2, 2025. They're available a la carte or as part of a combo meal. Prices vary by location, but at a location in Atlanta, a single taco costs $3.99, while three tacos and a drink cost $11.29.
Photo via Zaxbys.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Chicken Finger Tacos Return to Zaxbys with a New Flavor

M&M's adds a sweet and salty twist on its classic Peanut variety with the launch of new Honey Roasted Peanut M&M's.

A permanent addition to the brand's candy lineup, the new variety features the same peanut covered in milk chocolate and a candy shell, only with an added sweet and salty, honey-roasted flavor.
The new flavor comes in several sizes, including Single Size (1.63 oz), Share Size (2.83 oz), and Sharing Size stand-up pouches (8.6 oz). Honey Roasted M&M's will be available at retailers nationwide and on the M&M's website starting this month.
Photo via M&M's.

Checkers & Rally's introduces their own take on onion rings with the debut of new Fry-Seasoned Onion Fries.

Available for a limited time, the new menu item features fry-shaped (i.e., strips of onion) pieces of onion coated in Famous Seasoned Fries seasoning and then fried until crispy.
An order costs $3.49 (may vary).
Checkers & Rally's have a habit of adding their Famous Seasoned Fries seasoning to other menu items such as Fry-Seasoned Monsterella Stix and Fry-Seasoned Tenders.

articles.description:
White Castle kicks off the fall with the launch of the new BBQ Pulled Pork Slider, Triple Cheese Tots, and Pumpkin Latte Cheesecake-on-a-stick.

The BBQ Pulled Pork Slider features slow-roasted pulled pork smoked with hickory wood, mixed with a sweet BBQ sauce, and served with two pickle slices on a slider bun. You can get it a la carte or as part of a new $4.99 combo meal that includes two BBQ Pulled Pork Sliders, small fries, and a small drink.
New Triple Cheese Tots are fried tater tots infused with bits of cheddar, Colby Jack, and mozzarella cheese.  You get eight pieces per order.
The new Pumpkin Latte Cheesecake-on-a-stick features a slice of pumpkin latte cheesecake on a crushed graham cracker and brown sugar crust served on a stick.
All three menu items are available now for a limited time at participating locations.
Photo via White Castle.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: White Castle Launches New BBQ Pulled Pork Slider and More

The limited-time flavor features yeast ring donuts covered in a milk chocolate glaze. It is available by the dozen or individually.
This is the second time that Krispy Kreme has brought back the Chocolate Original Glazed Donuts this year.
As a promotion for the limited-time run, if you buy any dozen donuts at the regular price, you can get 50% off a dozen Chocolate Original Glazed Donuts. The deal is available when ordering in-person or online (using the code "CHOCOLATE").

articles.description:
The Pumpkin Pie Blizzard has returned to the Dairy Queen menu for the fall, accompanied by two new treats: the Maple Cookie Shake and the Caramel Toffee Cookie Blizzard.

The Pumpkin Pie Blizzard features the chain's signature vanilla soft serve mixed with pumpkin pie pieces, crowned with whipped topping and a dusting of nutmeg.
The new Maple Cookie Shake takes vanilla soft serve, blends it with a maple cookie crumble and milk, and finishes it off with whipped topping.
The new Caramel Toffee Cookie Blizzard features vanilla soft serve blended with chewy cookie pieces, toffee, and caramel.
The trio of seasonal items is available now for a limited time at participating locations nationwide.
Dairy Queen is also offering a limited-time deal through September 21, 2025, where DQ Rewards members can get a small Blizzard for 85 cents with a purchase of $1 or more through the DQ mobile app.
Photo via Dairy Queen.

articles.description:
Heading into fall, Shake Shack launches a new, limited-time French onion soup-inspired menu headlined by the French Onion Soup Burger.

The menu is available at Shake Shacks nationwide on September 12, 2025, but is available now if you order through the Shake Shack website or mobile app.
Shake Shack's French Onion Soup Burger features one or more quarter-pound Angus beef patties topped with Gruyere cheese, caramelized onions, crispy-fried sweet onions, and roasted garlic parmesan aioli on a toasted potato bun. The burger is available with up to three beef patties and starts at $11.79 with one patty here in Los Angeles.
Other items on the French Onion Menu include:
- French Onion 'Shroom Burger - A crisp-fried portobello mushroom filled with melted Muenster and cheddar cheeses, topped with caramelized onions and roasted garlic parmesan aioli on a toasted potato bun. It costs $11.79 near me.
- French Onion Shack Stack - A quarter-pound Angus beef patty topped with Gruyere cheese, a 'Shroom Burger patty, caramelized onions, and roasted garlic parmesan aioli on a toasted potato bun. It costs $15.29 at my local Shake Shack.
- Onion Rings - Crispy-fried, beer-battered sweet Spanish onion rings served with your choice of sauce. An order is $5.49 here.
- Parmesan Garlic Fries - Crinkle-cut fries dusted with parmesan cheese and served with a side of roasted garlic parmesan aioli. It costs $5.99 near me.
Photo via Shake Shack.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Shake Shack Debuts New French Onion Menu

Formerly called the "Apple Caramel Cheesecake," the Caramel Apple Cheesecake Cup features cheesecake swirled with a sweet and tangy caramel apple filling on a buttery graham cracker crust.
The limited-time dessert joins a selection that includes the Cinnamon Apple Pie and Strawberry Cream Cheese Pie.
Prices vary, but a location here in Los Angeles sells the seasonal dessert for $3.79.

Prices vary, but a location here in Los Angeles sells the fall cookies for $3.69 a piece (versus $3.29 for the other Classic cookies).

Taco Bell adds new Grande Nachos to the menu for a limited time. The spiritual successor of the Grande Nacho Box features a double portion of meat with nacho cheese and various toppings served in a paper box.

You can get the nachos with seasoned beef or slow-roasted chicken.
The Grande Nachos with Seasoned Beef includes a double portion of seasoned ground beef (compared to Nachos BellGrande), refried beans, warm nacho cheese sauce, a three-cheese blend (mozzarella, pepper jack, and cheddar), pico de gallo, reduced-fat sour cream, and guacamole, served over a bed of tortilla chips for a suggested price of $5.99.
The Grande Nachos with Slow-Roasted Chicken is the same, only with a double portion of slow-roasted chicken (versus the Cantina Chicken Soft Taco). It carries a suggested price of $6.99.
The prices can vary. One Taco Bell near me sells the Seasoned Beef version for $7.89, while the Slow-Roasted Chicken version is $8.89, but another location offers them for the advertised prices of $5.99 and $6.99.
Photo via Taco Bell.

The Pumpkin Spice Cappy features a blend of Arabica coffee, Pumpkin Cheesecake ice cream, and ice, topped with whipped cream and cinnamon.
Baskin-Robbins' Pumpkin Cheesecake ice cream is the Flavor of the Month for September 2025 and features a combination of pumpkin and cheesecake ice creams with a cinnamon cream cheese swirl and gingersnap cookie pieces.

articles.description:
Carne Asada is back on the menu at Chipotle for a limited time in the US and Canada.

First introduced in 2019 and returning for the fourth time, Chipotle's Carne Asada features marinated steak that's seasoned on the grill with a "signature" spice blend that includes cumin, coriander, garlic, and oregano. After cooking, it's sliced (rather than cubed like the chain's regular steak) and finished with fresh-squeezed lime and chopped cilantro.
Prices vary, but at my local Chipotle in Los Angeles, a Carne Asada Burrito Bowl costs $13.10, while a Steak Burrito Bowl is $11.70 (a Chicken, Veggie, or Sofritas Bowl is cheapest at $9.95).
In other news, Chipotle marks the start of football season by introducing its "Wear Your College Football Jersey" promotion on Monday, September 15, 2025, after 5PM (local time). During the promotion, you can get a buy-one-get-one-free deal on any entree if you show up wearing a college football jersey,
Finally, as a launch promo for the return of Carne Asada, through Tuesday, September 30, 2025, you can redeem a $0 delivery fee offer on all Carne Asada orders placed through the Chipotle website or mobile app.
Photo via Chipotle.
Read more at Brand Eating!

The Ultimate Cheesesteak Pizza is only available for ordering online. It is a large round pizza topped with cheesy white sauce, mozzarella, steak, onions, green peppers, and mushrooms. Pricing for the pizza starts at $14.99 (it's $16.99 here in Los Angeles).
Both menu items are available now for a limited time during the NFL season.
Photo via Little Caesars.

articles.title: Little Caesars Introduces New Cheesesteak Crazy Puffs

The Double Sonic Smasher is the chain's take on a smashed burger. It features two seasoned and seared Angus beef patties served with American cheese, Sonic Smasher sauce, crinkle-cut pickles, and diced onions on a toasted potato bun.

Jimmy John's debuts new Toasted Pizza Sandwiches as a limited-time special that builds off of its Toasted Sandwiches platform.

The new offering comes in two varieties:

- Toasted Three Cheese Pizza Sandwich - Mozzarella, provolone, parmesan,  marinara, and a dash of oregano-basil toasted on French bread. This one is the first vegetarian option for Jimmy John's Toasted Sandwich platform.
The new subs are the first limited-time offering for Jimmy John's Toasted Sandwich line, which was introduced earlier this year. Both pizza sandwiches are available now for a limited time at participating locations nationwide.
Prices vary, but a regular-sized sandwich of either variety costs $11.69 at one location here in Los Angeles.
Photo via Jimmy John's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Jimmy John’s Debuts New Toasted Pizza Sandwiches

articles.description:
Goldfish Dunkin' Pumpkin Spice Grahams are back in stores for a limited time in time for fall 2025.

The Goldfish x Dunkin' collaboration features fish-shaped graham crackers with the flavors of pumpkin, donut glaze, and pumpkin pie spices (including cinnamon, cloves, and nutmeg).
You can find them now nationwide at retailers such as Target, Walmart, and Amazon for a suggested price of $3.69 per 6.1-oz bag. They range from $2.99 to $3.49 near me in Los Angeles.
Photo via Goldfish.

articles.description:
Crumbl introduces a new "Secret Menu" item that changes each week to go with the rest of this week's dessert menu. The concept is similar to the Mystery Cookies that the chain occasionally offered. You can find out which Secret Menu item is available near you by checking the Crumbl website or mobile app.

In addition to the new Secret Menu, here's what's baking at Crumbl from September 8 through September 13, 2025:
- Cookies & Cream Cake featuring Oreo - A two-tiered dark chocolate cake made with Oreo cookie pieces, layered with Oreo cream cheese frosting, filled with an Oreo syrup, and topped with a chocolate glaze and a Mini Oreo cookie. 
- Confetti Milkshake Cookie - A chilled confetti sugar cookie rolled in rainbow sprinkles and topped with cake-flavored buttercream and a whipped cream rosette.

- Salted Caramel Cheesecake Cookie - A chilled graham cracker cookie rolled in graham cracker crumbs and topped with cheesecake frosting, a layer of caramel, and a sprinkle of sea salt.
- Milk Chocolate Chip Cookie - A warm cookie packed with milk chocolate chips.
Photo via Crumbl.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Crumbl Welcomes New "Secret Menu" Item and More Through September 13, 2025

articles.description:
Trader Joe's Strawberry & Cream Cloud Cake features "two layers of sponge cake with custard filling decorated with whipped cream and strawberries."

It's found in the frozen section, and a 17.64-oz cake cost me $6.49.

I thawed the cake in the fridge overnight per the instructions on the box, but this is what it looked like still frozen:



And here's how it looked after thawing:



While the Strawberry & Cream Cloud Cake comes from Sweden, it's very similar to an Asian-style strawberry cake. Mostly from the combination of fluffy sponge cake, whipped cream, and strawberries, but also because it wasn't too sweet. There were two key differences, however.



First, the cake is slightly sweeter than a similar Asian-style strawberry cake, as the strawberries are mixed with a sweet sauce here, versus just fresh strawberries. Given that they are frozen, the texture is different, but I enjoyed the sweetness and tart contrast between the strawberries and the rest of the cake.
Secondly, there's the layer of custard. It was a very thin layer and didn't make much of an impression on the custard front. What it did do was serve as a delivery vehicle for an almond flavor similar to marzipan.



Overall, Trader Joe's Strawberries & Cream Cloud Cake made for a light and refreshing dessert that really embraces its name. It's especially nice if you prefer your cakes on the less sweet side.
Nutritional Info - Trader Joe's Strawberries & Cream Cloud Cake
Serving Size - 1/6 cake (83g)
Calories - 160
Fat - 7g (Saturated Fat - 4g)
Sodium - 100mg
Carbs - 23g (Sugar - 13g)
Protein - 2g
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Review: Trader Joe's - Strawberries & Cream Cloud Cake

articles.description:
Hostess has brought back a trio of seasonal treats for fall 2025, including Maple Glazed Donettes and Pumpkin Spice Twinkies.

Maple Glazed Donettes are mini cake donuts covered in a maple-flavored glaze. You can find them now in stores nationwide in a 9.5-oz bag. Prices vary, but they cost $2.50 to $3 near me in Los Angeles.
Pumpkin Spice Twinkies come in a 10.86-oz box of eight individually-wrapped snack cakes. They feature the brand's classic golden sponge cake with a pumpkin spice-flavored creme. A box ranges from $3.50 to $5 near me.
Also back this year are Iced Pumpkin Cupcakes. They consist of pumpkin cupcakes with creme filling, topped with white icing and an orange icing squiggle. They're available in a 2-count single-serve pack and an 11.57-oz box of eight cupcakes. Like the Twinkies, the price of a box ranges from $3.50 to $5 near me.
Photo via Hostess.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Hostess Brings Back Maple Glazed Donettes and More for Fall 2025

articles.description:
Wingstop welcomes new Smoky Chipotle Rub as the latest limited-time flavor for the fast-food chain's various fried chicken options.

According to Wingstop, the new flavor takes its cues from the "classic smoky BBQ taste with a kick of fire-roasted peppers" and also includes a touch of honey and paprika. In terms of spiciness, it rates three "flames" out of five on Wingstop's heat scale.
Smoky Chipotle Rub is available now for a limited time at participating Wingstop locations nationwide. It can be ordered as a flavor on the chain's traditional wings, boneless wings, crispy strips, and chicken sandwiches.
Photo via Wingstop.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: New Smoky Chipotle Rub Arrives at Wingstop
